zh.lifeSummary: Pons IV de FOS de Peynier VIscomte de Marselha是Geoffroy I de Marseille和Rixinda de Narbonne Vescomtessa consort de Marselha的兒子，於1058年出生。他和Garrejade Pons III de Peynier de Marseille至少有6個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1131年6月11日享年73歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pons
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Pedro, Ramon, Guillermo, Roberto, Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Mario, Rafael, Apolonia.  French Adrien, Armand, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Marcelle, Matilde.
Catalan, French (Occitan), and Dutch: from the medieval personal name Pons (see Ponce ).
German: patronymic from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name, such as Bonhard, formed with the element bon ‘request, petition’ (compare Latin bonus).

variants.name: Ponce
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Luis, Jesus, Carlos, Francisco, Jorge, Miguel, Pedro, Raul, Roberto.

Spanish and French: from the medieval personal name Ponce, ultimately from Pontius, a Roman surname, perhaps an ethnic name for someone from Pontus (named from Greek pontos ‘ocean’) in Asia Minor, or an Italic cognate of Latin Quintus ‘fifth’ (i.e. ‘fifth-born’). The name was borne by two 3rd-century Christian saints, a Carthaginian deacon and a martyr of Nice, but was not widely popular in the Middle Ages because of the inhibiting influence of the even more famous Pontius Pilate. In some cases though, the surname may have been originally used for someone who had played the part of this character in a religious play.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: Pons IV de FOS de Peynier VIscomte de Marselha was born in 1058 as the son of Geoffroy I de Marseille and Rixinda de Narbonne Vescomtessa consort de Marselha. He had at least 6 sons and 1 daughter with Garrejade Pons III de Peynier de Marseille. He died on 11 June 1131, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Pons IV de FOS de Peynier VIscomte de Marselha est né en 1058, fils de Geoffroy I de Marseille et Rixinda de Narbonne Vescomtessa consort de Marselha. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille avec Garrejade Pons III de Peynier de Marseille. Il est décédé le 11 juin 1131, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Pons IV de FOS de Peynier VIscomte de Marselha wurde 1058, als Sohn von Geoffroy I de Marseille und Rixinda de Narbonne Vescomtessa consort de Marselha geboren. Er hatte mit Garrejade Pons III de Peynier de Marseille mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 11. Juni 1131 im Alter von 73 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Pons IV de FOS de Peynier VIscomte de Marselha è nato il nel 1058 come figlio di Geoffroy I de Marseille e Rixinda de Narbonne Vescomtessa consort de Marselha. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia con Garrejade Pons III de Peynier de Marseille. Morì il 11 giugno 1131, all'età di 73 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Pons IV de FOS de Peynier VIscomte de Marselhaは1058年にGeoffroy I de MarseilleとRixinda de Narbonne Vescomtessa consort de Marselhaの息子として生まれた。彼はGarrejade Pons III de Peynier de Marseilleとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1131年6月11日に73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Pons IV de FOS de Peynier VIscomte de Marselha(은)는 1058년 Geoffroy I de Marseille(와)과 Rixinda de Narbonne Vescomtessa consort de Marselha의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Garrejade Pons III de Peynier de Marseille(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1131년 6월 11일 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Pons IV de FOS de Peynier VIscomte de Marselha nasceu em 1058 filho de Geoffroy I de Marseille e Rixinda de Narbonne Vescomtessa consort de Marselha. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha com Garrejade Pons III de Peynier de Marseille. Ele faleceu em 11 de junho de 1131, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Pons IV de FOS de Peynier VIscomte de Marselha родился в 1058 г. Его родителей звали Geoffroy I de Marseille и Rixinda de Narbonne Vescomtessa consort de Marselha. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь от Garrejade Pons III de Peynier de Marseille. Он умер 11 июня 1131 г. в возрасте 73 лет.
